_meta.purpose: Frozen model comparison artifact for portfolio review. Every number traces to a saved training run.
_meta.experiment_tracking: mlflow.db (sqlite) — full hyperparameter sweep, including XGBoost depth variants, logged as MLflow runs
_meta.dataset: DataCo Smart Supply Chain — 180,519 order line items / 65,752 orders
_meta.primary_metric: F1 (55/45 class split makes accuracy misleading)

temporal.note: Headline split — orders sorted by date, train on past / test on future.

random.note: Stratified random split used only to confirm temporal stability.

xgboost_default_temporal._confusion_matrix_note: The confusion_matrix stored under this key in data/metrics/full_run_metrics.json ([[5234,707],[3361,3849]]) reconciles to precision 0.8448 / recall 0.5338 — i.e. it is the RANDOM-split matrix, not the temporal one. The scalar precision/recall/F1 above are internally consistent (2PR/(P+R)=0.6648) and are authoritative for the temporal split. CM omitted here to avoid propagating the mislabel.

xgboost_default_random.note: Random-split result reported in README comparison table. Close to temporal F1 (0.6648) — confirms no temporal degradation.

calibration.note: Poorly calibrated out of the box; isotonic calibration adapter added (Phase 6).

operating_point.rationale: False negatives (missed late order) cost ~3x a false positive (unneeded review). Lowering threshold to 0.35 raises recall from ~0.58 to ~0.80.

headline_finding: XGBoost adds no meaningful lift over logistic regression (F1 0.6648 vs 0.6531). Both models concentrate importance on shipping_mode, which the EDA shows is near-deterministic of lateness (First Class 95.3% late, Second Class 76.7%). The defensible production answer is a shipping-mode rule plus a model only for the ambiguous Standard Class tier. See docs/LIMITATIONS_AND_DECISIONS.md.
